digest.component_or_area: impact_code pass-2 impact model (code-review consequence binaries)
propositions: The impact_code pass-2 model has no vocabulary to express that a change introduces or unmasks a reachable code path with no automated test coverage, so such findings score impact 0.0 and no threshold can catch them.
A change lacking test coverage (change #529's advisory) scored severity none / impact 0.0 yet became a red-main incident shortly after merge, requiring fix #530.

Add a new abstain-safe maintainability-lane consequence binary bool reachable_path_without_automated_coverage at tier moderate (0.6) to _CODE_MAINT_BINARIES, the verify severity model, and the code-review-verify reviewer definition.
Per ADR 0036 a formula/vocabulary change requires bumping IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION from code-v2 to code-v3, updating the version-pinning test assertion accordingly.
Add a HIGH-labeled fixture row and a unit test replaying a #529-shaped finding that asserts impact > 0 under code-v3 while binary-absent code-v2 sidecars remain byte-unchanged.
This ticket fixes what pass 2 can express and is distinct from blocking-lane stories that tune pass-3 thresholds.
